ORDER DENYING RESPONDENT'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT BRONCO WINE CO.'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE OF THIS PETITION FOR ARBITRATION IS DENIED. VENUE FOR AN ARBITRATION PETITION IS GOVERNED BY CCP 1292, WHICH AUTHORIZES VENUE IN A COUNTY WHERE THE AGREEMENT IS TO BE PERFORMED. PETITIONER MPI BRANDS, INC. SHOWS THAT BRONCO WAS TO MAKE PAYMENTS UNDER THE AGREEMENT IN SAN FRANCISCO AND IN FACT MADE SOME SUCH PAYMENTS. (PATANE DEC. 5, EX. A; ANAHEIM EXTRUSION CO. V. SUP. CT. (1985) 170 CAL.APP.3D 1201.) NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MILES LENDWAY, CSR#13684 EMAIL: MLENDWAY@SFTC.ORG =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of Order Or, Alternatively, Appointment Of Arbitrator Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 1281.3 And 1281.6
Off Calendar Jul-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Attys. RYAN J. STONEROCK, Esq. of Harder LLP (Tel: 424-203-1600) & MARC S. MAZER, Esq. of Weill & Mazer (Tel: 415-421-0730) for Petitioner MPL Brands, Inc.;
Atty. BRANDY L. BARNES, Esq. of Rodarakis & Sousa, APC (Tel: 209-554-5232) for Respondent Bronco Wine Company.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT BRONCO WINE COMPANY'S Motion For Clarification Of Order Or, Alternatively, Appointment Of Arbitrator Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 1281.3 And 1281.6

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. This court's order compelling Magave Tequila, Inc. to arbitration needs no clarification. Magave was ordered to participate in the already pending arbitration. Both sides' papers on the motion to compel repeatedly referred to "the arbitration" and "this arbitration." No reference was made to Magave participating in any other arbitration. Thus, Bronco Wine Co.'s "motion for clarification of order, or, alternatively, appointment of arbitrator pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 1281.3 and 1281.6" is moot.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Sealing Records
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marc Mazer, Esq. 415-421-0730 of Weill & Mazer for Petitioner MPL Brands and Respondent Magave Tequila.
Kenneth Valinoti, Esq. 415-986-1338 x135 of Valinoti Specter & Dito LLP for Respondent MPL Brand -NV
Ryan Stonerock, Esq. 424-203-1600 of Harder LLP for Petitioner MPL brands through Court Call.
Eric Sousa, Esq. 209-554-5232 for Respondent Bronco Wine Company.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT BRONCO WINE COMPANY'S Motion For Order Sealing Records.

Bronco Wine Co.'s motion for order sealing records is denied. Court records are presumed to be open and Bronco fails to make the showings required for sealing. (CRC 2.550(d).) Further, the agreement Bronco seeks to seal has been in the public record for a year, so any confidentiality has been waived. (See Savaglio v. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 588, 598-601.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

PETITIONER MPL BRANDS, INC'S Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration Pursuant To Written Agreement And C.C.P.1281.2 And Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator Pursuant To C.C.P.1281.6.

OFF CALENDAR. The court executed the parties' stipulation and order that compels arbitration and takes this motion off calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Respondent Bronco Wine Co.'s motion to change venue of this petition for arbitration is DENIED. Venue for an arbitration petition is governed by CCP 1292, which authorizes venue in a county where the agreement is to be performed. Petitioner MPI Brands, Inc. shows that Bronco was to make payments under the agreement in San Francisco and in fact made some such payments. (Patane Dec. 5, Ex. A; Anaheim Extrusion Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 1201.) No sanctions are awarded.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Miles Lendway, CSR#13684 email: mlendway@sftc.org =(302/RBU)
